none: FAP-3, Milwaukee County expressway system construction to connect I-94 stadium interchange and I-894 Greenfield interchange, Milwaukee County : environmental impact statement.

The proposed project involves constructing a five-mile section of the Milwaukee County Expressway System, connecting the Stadium Freeway and the Greenfield Interchange. This new six-lane freeway aims to improve transportation in Milwaukee County, Wisconsin, specifically in the cities of Milwaukee, Greenfield, and the Village of West Milwaukee. The project is expected to displace some residential, commercial, and park areas, affecting air quality and noise levels. However, it promises to enhance traffic flow and economic activity in the region.
FAP-3, Milwaukee County expressway system construction to connect I-94 stadium interchange and I-894 Greenfield interchange, Milwaukee County : environmental impact statement.

The proposed project involves constructing a five-mile section of the Milwaukee County Expressway System, connecting the Stadium Freeway and the Greenfield Interchange. This new six-lane freeway aims to improve transportation in Milwaukee County, Wisconsin, specifically in the cities of Milwaukee, Greenfield, and the Village of West Milwaukee. The project is expected to displace some residential, commercial, and park areas, affecting air quality and noise levels. However, it promises to enhance traffic flow and economic activity in the region.
Milwaukee County Expressway and Transportation Commission

The FAP-3 project was proposed to alleviate traffic congestion and improve transportation efficiency in Milwaukee County. While there was some public concern regarding the displacement of residents and the potential environmental impacts, the project was seen as necessary for economic growth in the area. Ultimately, the project moved forward to enhance connectivity and support regional development.

"The official position of the Village Board is in opposition to the proposed freeway on the basis that it would not be required to serve traffic needs of the village for ten to twenty years." — West Milwaukee Hearing - June 20, 1955
"The community has selected and consistently supported the recommended plan." — Public Meeting - June 6, 1967
